subject: Human Resources
title: Exempt vs. Non-Exempt Employees
description: A short lesson to help you apply Exempt vs. Non-Exempt Employees.

hook.text: A 'manager' at a fast food chain works 55 hours a week with no overtime. But their job is mostly making food, not managing.

buildup.text: Exempt employees are salaried and not entitled to overtime. Non-exempt employees must receive overtime pay.

discovery.text: Exemption requires meeting salary thresholds AND duties tests—executive, administrative, professional, or outside sales.

twist.text: Job title doesn't determine exemption—duties do. Calling someone a 'manager' doesn't make them exempt if they don't manage.

climax.text: Misclassifying non-exempt workers as exempt triggers class-action lawsuits—some costing hundreds of millions in back pay.

punchline.text: The title says manager. The law asks: what do you actually do?

quiz.question: What determines exempt status under the FLSA?
options: Meeting both salary thresholds and specific duties tests
Being paid a salary of any amount
